additional_problems.description: Master sliding window technique for array/string problems

new_problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters (Variant)
new_problems.skill_focus: Sliding window with hash map tracking
new_problems.description: Find the longest substring without repeating characters. Use a sliding window approach with character frequency tracking.
new_problems.hint: Maintain a window [left, right] and expand right. When duplicate found, shrink from left until duplicate removed.
new_problems.approach: Use a hash map to track character positions. When you encounter a character already in the window, move left pointer to skip the previous occurrence.

new_problems.title: Max Consecutive Ones III
new_problems.description: Given a binary array and an integer k, find the maximum number of consecutive 1's you could create by flipping at most k 0's.
new_problems.hint: Expand window while counting zeros. When zeros exceed k, contract from left.
new_problems.approach: Use sliding window to track a subarray with at most k zeros. Expand right pointer and count zeros. When count exceeds k, shrink from left.

new_problems.title: Minimum Window Substring (Variant)
new_problems.skill_focus: Sliding window with multiple character requirements
new_problems.description: Given two strings s and t, find the minimum window in s that contains all characters in t. Characters in t can repeat.
new_problems.hint: Use two hash maps - one for target chars, one for window chars. Expand until valid, then contract to find minimum.
new_problems.approach: Maintain a frequency map of target characters. Use sliding window to find substrings containing all required characters. Optimize by contracting from left when valid.

new_problems.title: Container With Most Water (Variant)
new_problems.description: Given an integer array height where each element represents a vertical line's height, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that holds the most water.
new_problems.hint: Start with widest container. Move the pointer pointing to shorter line inward.
new_problems.approach: Use two pointers at start and end. Calculate area and move the pointer at shorter height inward (to potentially find taller lines).

new_problems.title: Rotate Array
new_problems.description: Rotate an array to the right by k steps. Try to do it in-place with O(1) extra space.
new_problems.hint: Try reversing segments of the array. Reverse first k elements, then remaining, then entire array.
new_problems.approach: Reverse the last k elements, reverse the first n-k elements, then reverse the entire array.

new_problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
new_problems.skill_focus: Array traversal with optimal state tracking
new_problems.description: Find the maximum profit you can get by buying and selling stocks. You must buy before you sell, and you can complete at most one transaction.
new_problems.hint: Track the minimum price seen so far and calculate profit at each step.
new_problems.approach: Maintain minimum price seen so far. At each position, calculate potential profit and update maximum.

additional_problems.description: Efficient searching techniques using binary search

new_problems.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
new_problems.description: Search for a target value in a rotated sorted array. The array was originally sorted in ascending order then rotated at an unknown pivot.
new_problems.hint: Determine which half is sorted, then check if target is in that range.
new_problems.approach: Use binary search. At each step, identify which half is properly sorted. Check if target is in the sorted half, then adjust search accordingly.

new_problems.title: Find First and Last Position of Element in Sorted Array
new_problems.description: Given a sorted array and a target value, find the starting and ending position of the target. If not found, return [-1, -1].
new_problems.hint: Run binary search twice - once for leftmost, once for rightmost position.
new_problems.approach: Perform two binary searches: one to find leftmost occurrence, one to find rightmost occurrence.

new_problems.title: Peak Index in a Mountain Array
new_problems.description: An array is a mountain if it strictly increases then strictly decreases. Find the peak index (maximum value's index).
new_problems.hint: Compare arr[mid] with neighbors. Peak is where left < mid > right.
new_problems.approach: Use binary search by comparing mid element with neighbors. If mid is less than right, go right. Otherwise, go left.

additional_problems.description: Graph traversal techniques - Depth First and Breadth First Search

new_problems.title: Number of Islands (Variant)
new_problems.description: Given a 2D grid of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands. An island is surrounded by water and connected diagonally and orthogonally.
new_problems.hint: Use DFS to mark all connected land cells as visited. Increment counter for each new island found.
new_problems.approach: Iterate through grid. When finding '1', perform DFS to mark all connected '1's as visited and increment island count.

new_problems.title: Clone Graph
new_problems.description: Create a deep copy of an undirected graph. Each node contains a value and a list of neighbors.
new_problems.hint: Use a hash map to track visited nodes and their clones. Use DFS to traverse and clone.
new_problems.approach: Maintain a map of original nodes to cloned nodes. Use DFS to traverse original graph, creating cloned nodes and connecting them.

new_problems.title: Course Schedule (Topological Sort)
new_problems.skill_focus: DFS for cycle detection and ordering
new_problems.description: Given courses and prerequisites, determine if you can finish all courses. Return order if possible.
new_problems.hint: Use DFS with three states: unvisited, visiting, visited. Detect cycles during traversal.
new_problems.approach: Build adjacency list. Use DFS with color coding (white/gray/black) to detect cycles and produce topological order.

new_problems.title: Word Ladder
new_problems.description: Find the shortest transformation sequence from start word to end word, where each intermediate word must exist in a word list and differ by exactly one letter.
new_problems.hint: Use BFS from start word. For each word, find all neighbors (differ by one letter) and explore them level by level.
new_problems.approach: Build graph implicitly by finding neighbors (words differing by one char). Use BFS to find shortest path from start to end.

additional_problems.description: In-order, Pre-order, Post-order and Level-order tree traversals

new_problems.title: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
new_problems.description: Find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two nodes in a binary search tree. Both nodes will exist in the tree.
new_problems.hint: Use BST property: if both values are less than root, go left; if both greater, go right; otherwise root is LCA.
new_problems.approach: Traverse from root. If both node values are less than current node, go left. If both greater, go right. When they split, that's the LCA.

new_problems.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
new_problems.description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree. A path can start and end at any nodes in the tree.
new_problems.hint: For each node, calculate max path sum going through it. Track global maximum.
new_problems.approach: Use DFS post-order traversal. For each node, calculate max path sum with node as highest point and also max path extending from node.

new_problems.title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
new_problems.description: Serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize the string back to the tree. Preserve structure.
new_problems.hint: Use pre-order traversal for serialization. Use markers (like '#') for null nodes.
new_problems.approach: Serialize using pre-order DFS with null markers. Deserialize by parsing string and reconstructing tree using pre-order traversal.

additional_problems.description: Optimization problems using memoization and tabulation

new_problems.title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
new_problems.description: Find the length of the longest increasing subsequence. Elements don't need to be contiguous.
new_problems.hint: dp[i] = length of LIS ending at index i. For each i, check all j < i where arr[j] < arr[i].
new_problems.approach: Define dp[i] as LIS length ending at i. For each position, find max dp[j] where arr[j] < arr[i] and add 1.

new_problems.title: Coin Change
new_problems.description: Given coins of different denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins needed. Return -1 if impossible.
new_problems.hint: dp[i] = minimum coins to make amount i. For each amount, try using each coin.
new_problems.approach: Use DP where dp[i] represents min coins for amount i. For each amount, try each coin and take minimum.

new_problems.title: Word Break II
new_problems.description: Given a string and word list, return all possible sentences where the string is split into words from the list.
new_problems.hint: Use DP to identify valid split points, then backtrack to build all possible combinations.
new_problems.approach: Memoize which substrings can be segmented. Then use backtracking to build all valid word break combinations.

additional_problems.description: Problems involving interval manipulation and merging

new_problems.title: Non-overlapping Intervals
new_problems.description: Given intervals, find the minimum number of intervals to remove so remaining intervals don't overlap.
new_problems.hint: Sort by end point. Greedily select intervals that don't overlap with previously selected.
new_problems.approach: Sort intervals by end time. Greedily select non-overlapping intervals and count removals.

new_problems.title: Insert Interval
new_problems.description: Insert a new interval into a list of non-overlapping intervals and merge if necessary.
new_problems.hint: Add new interval, then merge overlapping intervals like in merge intervals problem.
new_problems.approach: Handle three cases: intervals ending before new interval starts, overlapping intervals to merge, and intervals starting after new interval ends.

new_problems.title: Minimum Meeting Rooms
new_problems.description: Given meeting intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms needed.
new_problems.hint: Sort by start time. Use min-heap to track meeting end times. If new meeting starts after earliest end, reuse room.
new_problems.approach: Sort meetings by start time. Use min-heap of end times. For each meeting, check if can reuse room with earliest end time.

new_problems.title: 3Sum
new_problems.description: Find all unique triplets in array that sum to zero. Return all triplets without duplicates.
new_problems.hint: Sort array. For each element, use two pointers to find pairs summing to -element.
new_problems.approach: Sort array. For each number, use two pointers to find pairs that sum to -number. Skip duplicates carefully.

new_problems.title: Trapping Rain Water
new_problems.description: Given elevation map, calculate how much water can be trapped after raining.
new_problems.hint: Use two pointers from both ends. Track max heights seen. Water at each position = min(left_max, right_max) - height.
new_problems.approach: Two pointers from both ends. At each step, move pointer with smaller max height and calculate trapped water.

additional_problems.description: Problems involving finding sums with specific constraints

new_problems.title: Two Sum II - Input Array is Sorted
new_problems.skill_focus: Two pointer sum finding with sorted input
new_problems.description: Given a sorted array, find two numbers that add up to a target. Return indices (1-indexed).
new_problems.hint: Use two pointers from both ends. If sum is too small, move left pointer right. If too large, move right pointer left.
new_problems.approach: Two pointers from start and end. If sum equals target, return. If less, move left pointer. If more, move right pointer.

new_problems.title: Subarray Sum Equals K
new_problems.description: Find the number of subarrays with sum equal to k.
key_concepts: Hash map for cumulative sum tracking
new_problems.hint: Track cumulative sum. For each position, check if (current_sum - k) exists in hash map.
new_problems.approach: Use prefix sum with hash map. For each element, add current sum to map. Check if (current_sum - k) exists to find valid subarrays.

new_problems.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
new_problems.description: Given histogram bars, find the area of largest rectangle that can be formed.
new_problems.hint: Use monotonic stack to find left and right boundaries for each bar where it's the minimum.
new_problems.approach: Use monotonic stack to efficiently find left and right span for each height. Calculate max area.

new_problems.title: Daily Temperatures
new_problems.description: For each day's temperature, find how many days until a warmer temperature. Return array of days (0 if never).
new_problems.hint: Use stack to track temperatures in decreasing order. Pop when finding warmer temp.
new_problems.approach: Traverse temperatures. Maintain stack of indices in decreasing temp order. When finding warmer, pop and record day difference.

new_problems.title: Word Pattern
new_problems.description: Check if a string follows a pattern. Each character in pattern maps to unique word, and each word maps to unique character.
new_problems.hint: Maintain two mappings: character to word and word to character. They must be consistent.
new_problems.approach: Split words and validate pattern. Check bidirectional mapping consistency using two hash maps.

new_problems.title: Concatenated Words
new_problems.description: Find all words in list that can be formed by concatenating other words from the list.
key_concepts: DP or DFS with word set
new_problems.hint: For each word, check if it can be formed by combining other words using DP or DFS.
new_problems.approach: Sort by length. For each word, use DP to check if it can be formed from words before it in the list.

additional_problems.description: Problems involving binary operations and bit patterns

new_problems.title: Single Number III
new_problems.description: Given array with three identical numbers and two unique numbers, find the two unique numbers.
new_problems.hint: XOR all numbers. Result has bits set where two unique numbers differ. Use this to partition and find both.
new_problems.approach: XOR all to get XOR of two unique numbers. Find set bit to partition numbers. XOR partitions separately.

new_problems.title: Number of 1 Bits
new_problems.description: Count the number of 1 bits in binary representation of a number.
new_problems.hint: Use n & (n-1) to remove rightmost 1 bit. Repeat until n becomes 0. Count iterations.
new_problems.approach: Use bit manipulation: n & (n-1) removes rightmost set bit. Count how many iterations to reach 0.

additional_problems.description: Prefix tree data structure for string problems

new_problems.title: Word Search II
new_problems.description: Find all words from a word list that exist in a 2D board. Words can be formed by moving adjacent cells.
new_problems.hint: Build Trie from words. DFS on board, checking at each cell if prefix exists in Trie.
new_problems.approach: Build Trie of word list. DFS from each board cell, tracking path in Trie. When reaching word end in Trie, add to result.

new_problems.title: Replace Words
new_problems.skill_focus: Trie for prefix matching and replacement
new_problems.description: Given a dictionary and a sentence, replace words with their shortest root from dictionary.
new_problems.hint: Build Trie from dictionary. For each word in sentence, find shortest root using Trie.
new_problems.approach: Build Trie of roots. For each word, traverse Trie to find shortest root, or use word if no root found.

new_problems.title: Combinations of Phone Number
new_problems.description: Given digit string like '23', return all possible letter combinations (2->ABC, 3->DEF).
new_problems.hint: Use backtracking. For each digit, recursively build combinations with its mapped letters.
new_problems.approach: Map digits to letters. Use backtracking to generate all combinations by iterating through letters for each digit.

new_problems.title: Combination Sum
new_problems.description: Find all unique combinations that sum to a target. Each number can be used unlimited times.
new_problems.hint: Use backtracking. Start from index to avoid duplicates. Each number can be reused.
new_problems.approach: Backtrack with current sum. For each number, try including it (reuse same index) or moving to next number.

new_problems.title: Sudoku Solver
new_problems.description: Solve a Sudoku puzzle using backtracking. Fill empty cells following Sudoku rules.
new_problems.hint: For each empty cell, try digits 1-9. Check validity. Recurse if valid, backtrack if no solution.
new_problems.approach: Find empty cell. Try each digit. Check if valid (row/column/box). Recurse. Backtrack if needed.

new_problems.title: Generate Parentheses
new_problems.description: Generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses with n pairs.
new_problems.hint: Track open and close counts. Can add '(' if open < n. Can add ')' if close < open.
new_problems.approach: Backtrack with open and close parenthesis counts. Add '(' if count < n, ')' if close count < open count.

new_problems.title: Palindrome Partitioning
new_problems.description: Partition a string into all possible ways where each partition is a palindrome.
new_problems.hint: Backtrack from each position. Check if substring is palindrome before recursing.
new_problems.approach: For each starting index, find all palindrome substrings. Backtrack with remaining string. Build all partitions.

metadata.note: Each new problem is designed to strengthen the same DSA fundamentals as the original problem but with different problem scenarios and additional challenges.
